label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the remainder when 3^200 is divided by 13. Hmm, that's a pretty big exponent. I can't just calculate 3^200 directly because that's way too large. There must be a pattern or a theorem that can help me simplify this. Let me think... I remember something called Fermat's Little Theorem. Let me recall what that is. If p is a prime number and a is not divisible by p, then a^(p-1) ≡ 1 mod p. Right? So since 13 is a prime number and 3 isn't divisible by 13, this theorem should apply here. So according to Fermat's Little Theorem, 3^(12) ≡ 1 mod 13. That means every time the exponent is a multiple of 12, the result will be 1 modulo 13. Therefore, maybe I can break down 3^200 into parts where the exponents are multiples of 12? Let me try to express 200 divided by 12.
